entry.text: Jack is still at the helicopter when you find him again, forearms deep in an engine housing, humming to himself — something that might be a song or might just be the sound a person makes when they've found a problem they know how to fix. He looks up when you approach. His eyes go straight to the mask. Something in his face relaxes that you didn't notice was tense.

mask_delivered.text: 'There she is.' He puts it on immediately, over the romper, and the effect is — genuinely unsettling, actually. The mask is doing a lot of work. He nods, rolls his shoulders, and something in his posture changes. Then he gets back to the helicopter with a renewed intensity. Forty minutes of clanging and swearing later, he wheels a motorcycle out from behind the wreck — rusted, patched, re-fitted with components from the helicopter's systems. It starts on the second kick. He looks unreasonably proud. 'She's yours,' he says. 'You earned her.'

minor_complication.text: Jack gets the mask tangled in the romper's neck drawstring trying to put it on. You wait. It takes a while. No one speaks of it.
